title: Terminated Account Verbiage
content:
Overview


Account termination is the most serious action Tklesson can take and should only be communicated by a Lead or supervisor — never by a front-line agent. However, agents may receive inquiries from customers whose accounts have been terminated, or may need to document reports that could lead to termination. Handle these situations with professionalism and empathy, even when the customer has violated platform policies.



If a Customer Inquires About Their Terminated Account


Step 1 — Do not speculate or discuss reasons:


Front-line agents do not have access to termination details and should not attempt to explain why an account was terminated, even if the reason seems obvious.


"I can see your account has been flagged for review. I don't have access to the specific details from my side, but I can escalate this for you."


Step 2 — Document and escalate:


Create a ticket under Account category with the subject: "Terminated Account Inquiry — [customer email]". Include the customer's name, email, and a summary of their inquiry. Escalate to the Lead.


Step 3 — Set expectations:


"I've documented your inquiry and our Lead will review your account. They'll follow up with you via email within 24 hours with more information."


Do not:


  • Confirm or deny any specific reason for termination.
  • Promise reinstatement.
  • Argue with the customer about whether termination was justified.
  • Share internal policies about what leads to termination — this is confidential.


If You Witness Behavior That May Warrant Termination


Agents may encounter customers engaging in behavior that violates Tklesson's terms, including:


  • Harassment or abuse toward tutors, students, or support staff.
  • Fraudulent activity (payment disputes, credit manipulation, identity fraud).
  • Repeated violation of platform policies after warnings.
  • Sharing inappropriate content through chat or session platforms.
  • Using the platform for solicitation, spam, or commercial purposes unrelated to tutoring.

If you witness such behavior:


  1. Do not confront the customer directly about termination.
  2. Document the incident in detail — include timestamps, screenshots, and relevant chat or session IDs.
  3. Create a ticket under Account category with the subject: "Termination Review — [customer email] — [reason]".
  4. Escalate to the Lead immediately. Do not discuss the report with the customer.


Key Phrases for Agents


  • "I can see your account is under review. I'll escalate this to our Lead for you."
  • "I don't have access to the specifics, but our Lead will follow up within 24 hours."
  • "I've documented your inquiry and you'll receive a response soon."
  • Never say: "You were terminated because..." or "Our policy says..." — this is above your scope.
  • Never say: "I'm sure it's a mistake" — this creates false hope and undermines the process.


Emotional Support Without Overpromising


Customers inquiring about terminated accounts may be upset, confused, or angry. Acknowledge their feelings without making commitments:


"I understand this is frustrating, and I'm sorry you're in this position. I've made sure our Lead has all the information they need. They'll review everything carefully and get back to you."


If the customer becomes abusive, refer to the De-escalation article and do not hesitate to end the interaction if necessary.
